examples: The phonic wheel motor is invented by Poul la Cour in 1878.
His story is not phonic, but body language - either dace or magic.
Successful phonic work depends on children being able to listen well and speak clearly.
The government wants it to be used systematically - with children building up their phonic knowledge stage-by-stage.
Emphasis on multi-sensory learning: Individual phonic, sight word and rule cards reinforce learning, as do games and walk-around activities.
